exemplar.title: What are the challenges raised by the communication and/or dissemination of knowledge?

detailedExemplarData.title: What are the challenges raised by the communication and/or dissemination of knowledge?

strengths: Clear identification of three authentic, context-rich real-world objects
Well-explained links between each object and the chosen IA prompt
Purposeful engagement with TOK ideas supported by citations and external sources
Organizational coherence with effective section headers and logical progression
weaknesses: Occasional lapses into descriptive repetition rather than deeper analysis
Key TOK concepts (e.g., availability bias, authority bias) asserted without precise definition or integration
Factual inaccuracies (e.g., misspelling of âGoghâ) and incomplete thoughts undermine clarity
Justifications for each objectâs unique contribution could be tighter and narrative synthesis remains implicit
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition meets the 7â8 band descriptors by clearly identifying and situating three specific objects, making well-explained links to the IA prompt, and supporting most points with appropriate evidence and citations. However, lapses in analytical precision, factual accuracy, and depth of TOK concept integration prevent the work from reaching the consistently sophisticated standard required for a 9â10 score. A score of 7 appropriately reflects strong performance with room for tighter analysis and narrative cohesion.

annotations.comment: The analysis recognizes how authority figures can manipulate shared knowledge, effectively illustrating a real-world knowledge issue aligned with the prompt.

The student clearly situates Object 1 in its realâworld context by specifying the date, platform and political backdrop, effectively anchoring the example in the promptâs exploration of authority and dissemination.
The transition to Object 2 is clearly marked, maintaining structural coherence and signalling a purposeful progression through the exhibition.
The painterâs name is misspelled as âGophâ in places; ensure factual accuracy to maintain academic credibility when discussing historical objects.
The sentence ends abruptly at âevenâ, indicating an incomplete thought; ensure each analytical claim is fully developed to maintain argumentative clarity.
The caption âScreenshot of my Twitter pageâ is overly generic; a more precise caption noting source URL and timestamp would enhance credibility and traceability.

The discussion of Trumpâs authority asserts âcredibilityâ without referencing TOK concepts like âauthority biasâ or providing evidence for its psychological effect; integrating a precise definition would deepen analysis.
The source and date of the dictionary screenshot are missing; citing the website URL and access date would bolster the objectâs contextual authenticity.
The student mentions âavailability biasâ but does not define it within TOK or show how it directly applies; include a concise TOK definition and a concrete example link.
The cultural explanation of âmotochorroâ and its broader socioâhistorical significance is well supported by an external source, enriching the justification for its inclusion.
Including the actual screenshot of the tweet strengthens authenticity and allows readers to engage directly with the knowledge artifact under discussion.
This paragraph largely repeats earlier points about personal bias; streamline to avoid redundancy and allocate space for deeper TOK exploration.
While noting personal beliefs as a challenge, the student misses explicit linkage to the promptâs wording on âdissemination of knowledgeâ; restate the prompt terms to sharpen focus.
To strengthen analysis, the student could reference a specific theory or study on misinformation proliferation, rather than relying solely on general assertions.
The discussion of subjective interpretation is insightful but would benefit from a counterâexample where consensus exists, deepening the TOK analysis through contrast.
The section header for Object 3 is concise and immediately informs the reader of the next focus, preserving the exhibitionâs organizational clarity.
The analysis details subjective readings but omits explicit mention of the promptâs term âcommunicationâ; integrating prompt vocabulary would clarify the TOK focus.
The caption merely restates the content; it should instead highlight why this particular translation instance exemplifies the promptâs concern with cultural nuance.
While the image description is vivid, the student should consider how viewer background influences interpretation of brushwork and colourâa direct link to knowledgeâcommunication challenges.
Referencing van Gogh & Niemeyer (2024) demonstrates engagement with scholarly sources, reinforcing the argument about evidence gaps in art interpretation.
Ensure all electronic sources include access dates and URLs are hyperlinked or presented consistently to facilitate source verification.
The paragraph cuts off at âambiguity in theâ; ensure each key idea is completed and tied back to the central thesis for a cohesive narrative.
Reference entries display inconsistent formatting and missing retrieval dates; adopt a standardized citation style (e.g., APA or MLA) to enhance professionalism.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
